meta.title: Zizo Circle — 30-day social calendar (Qatar)
meta.audience: Doha-resident expats and young Qatari nationals, ages 22-35, smartphone-first, primarily English-speaking with significant Arabic / Hindi / Tagalog audiences
meta.tone: Warm, specific, low-key confident, multilingual-aware. Group-first, never dating-coded. No alcohol, no orientalist imagery.

pillars.name: Belonging
pillars.desc: Human stories about finding your people in Qatar

pillars.desc: How Zizo works, AI matching, cashback, safety

pillars.desc: Qatar lifestyle moments, multilingual Doha, anchor dates

pillars.name: Promotional
pillars.desc: Venue partners, referral, Zizo Nights, launches

scheduling_notes: Qatar work week is Sunday-Thursday. Friday-Saturday is the weekend.
All times listed in Arabia Standard Time (AST, UTC+3). No DST in Qatar.
Pause all paid push and notifications during 5 daily prayer windows. Use a Qatar prayer-time API.
Instagram and Snapchat are the primary discovery surfaces in Qatar; LinkedIn is founder/B2B credibility; Twitter has lower engagement here but is still useful for tech / journalist reach.
Best post times observed in GCC: LinkedIn Sun-Wed 08:00-10:00; Twitter Sun-Thu 12:00-13:00 and 20:00-22:00; Instagram Sun-Thu 19:00-21:00 (post-dinner peak).
meta.image_prompt_engine: Flux 1.1 Pro / Flux Dev — prompts written for editorial-realistic style; render 9:16 for Reels/Stories, 1:1 for grid. Always specify modest dress and Qatar setting.
review_required: All Arabic copy MUST be reviewed by a native Khaleeji speaker before publishing.
Image generations MUST be reviewed for cultural appropriateness before any are scheduled.
Run final pass against brand-identity-qatar.md §6 do/don't list.

days.theme: Best quiet brunch spots in Doha

linkedin.copy:
Doha has 200+ brunch spots. Most of them are loud, packed, and impossible to actually have a conversation in.

We asked 50 Zizo Circle users where they go when they actually want to *talk*. Three names came up over and over:

→ Toro Toro at The St. Regis (the patio, not the main room)
→ Sukar Pasha Ottoman Lounge in Souq Waqif
→ The garden side of Cafe #999 in Msheireb

What's the one place in Doha you'd take someone you actually want to listen to?

twitter.copy:
Quiet brunch in Doha — actually possible.

3 spots Zizo users keep going back to:
• Toro Toro patio @ St. Regis
• Sukar Pasha @ Souq Waqif
• Café #999 garden, Msheireb

Where do you go when you want to hear the other person?

instagram.caption:
Doha is loud. Brunch doesn't have to be.

Five places we send Zizo Circle users when they want to actually hear the person across the table. Save this one for next Friday morning.

Which would you add? ↓

#Doha #DohaBrunch #Qatar #QatarLife #ThingsToDoInDoha #DohaCafes

days.theme: How AI matching actually works (no magic, just interests)

linkedin.copy:
Most 'AI matching' apps are just swipe-and-pray with extra steps.

At Zizo Circle we built it differently. Three things go into a match:

1) Your interests (the 34 things we ask you about)
2) Your distance (people who can actually meet you in Doha tonight, not 90km away)
3) Your availability rhythm (do you go out Thursdays or Saturdays?)

No photos in the first pass. No height. No 'vibe.' Just whether you'd both have something to do together at 7pm on a Wednesday.

The match score is a number. We show it because the math should be visible.

twitter.copy:
Our 'AI match score' is just three things:

— how much your interests overlap
— how close you are right now
— whether your usual going-out time matches

No photos, no swiping, no vibe-guessing. The math is visible because it should be.

instagram.caption:
What the 94% actually means 👇

No magic. Just three things:
• interest overlap
• you're both nearby
• you both go out around the same time

The score is a real number. We show it because the math should be visible.

#ZizoCircle #Doha #Qatar #HowItWorks

linkedin.copy:
I moved to Doha three years ago.

My first week, I sat in a café in West Bay and realized I had no one to text. Not 'no friends yet' — I mean *no one*. The friend group I'd left in Manila had already moved on to a Friday night I wasn't part of.

I ended up on Bumble BFF. Met two great people. Both moved away within a year.

I started Zizo Circle for the version of me who landed at Hamad International with two suitcases and a contract. The app I wish someone had told me about on day one.

If you just landed: welcome. We'll find your people.

twitter.copy:
Just landed in Doha?

You will, at some point in your first month, sit in a café and realize you have no one to text.

This is the part nobody warns you about. We built Zizo Circle for that week.

instagram.caption:
The week nobody warns you about. 🇶🇦

If you just moved to Doha — this one's for you.

#ZizoCircle #Doha #Qatar #NewInDoha #ExpatLife

linkedin.copy:
Three things I plan every Thursday afternoon as a Doha resident:

1. Friday morning — quiet. Either Aspire Park walk or coffee at a place I know won't be packed.

2. Friday afternoon — nothing scheduled until after Asr. The city is asleep and that's the point.

3. Saturday — the social one. The brunch, the padel, the catch-up.

The rhythm of Doha weekends is something you only learn by living here. What's your Friday-Saturday shape look like?

twitter.copy:
The Doha weekend rhythm:

Thu evening — slow start
Fri morning — quiet
Fri afternoon — nothing until after Asr
Sat — the social day

Learned this the hard way. What's yours look like?

instagram.caption:
Doha weekend, mapped 🗺️

Four panels for the rhythm only locals figure out. Save it; share it with someone who just moved.

#ZizoCircle #DohaWeekend #Qatar #DohaLife

linkedin.copy:
Padel in Qatar grew from 4 courts in 2019 to over 200 in 2025. The bottleneck now isn't court availability — it's finding three other people at your level on a Thursday night.

This is exactly the discovery problem Zizo Circle was built for. Filter by sport, level, neighborhood, time window. We surface the four people nearby tonight whose match score with each other is high enough that the game will actually be good.

The Qatar Padel scene is one of the best examples of why interest-matching beats interest-listing.

twitter.copy:
Need a 4th for padel tonight in Doha?

Zizo Circle finds 3 nearby players at your level whose schedules match yours. Filter by court, time, language preference.

The game starts in 5 mins instead of 5 days of WhatsApp groups.

instagram.caption:
POV: it's Thursday night, you want to play padel, you have no fourth.

Tap. Match. Court booked. Game on. 🎾

#Padel #Doha #Qatar #ZizoCircle #PadelQatar

Find your fourth.']

linkedin.copy:
Going out in Doha isn't cheap. The average Zizo Circle user spends QAR 1,800/month on cafés, meals, padel, and social activities.

We think going out should give back. So every visit to a partner venue earns QR straight to your in-app wallet:

• Café visit: 5-10% back
• Restaurant: 8-15% back
• Padel court: 10-20% back
• Group bookings (3+): bonus QAR 25 boost

No points. No tier games. No 'expires in 30 days.' Just real Qatari Riyals you can spend at the next partner venue.

If you're a venue in Doha and this fits your model, we'd love to talk.

twitter.copy:
Cashback ≠ points.

Zizo Circle gives you actual QAR. Spend at a partner café, get 5-10% back in your wallet. Spend at a partner padel court, get 10-20% back.

No expiry. No tier games. Just QR you spend at the next venue.

instagram.caption:
+QAR 14 added. ✨

No points. No tiers. Just real Qatari Riyals you spend at the next partner venue.

Going out should give back.

#ZizoCircle #Cashback #Doha #Qatar

days.theme: First friend you make in Qatar

linkedin.copy:
There's a question I ask every Doha resident I meet: 'Who was the first real friend you made here?'

The answers are always specific. 'Aisha — we sat next to each other in a yoga class at Aspire.' 'Vinod, my colleague's brother-in-law.' 'A guy named Hassan who I met in line at Karak.'

Nobody ever says 'I made friends through Bumble BFF.' Or 'I posted in a Facebook group.'

The first real friend always shows up sideways — through a thing you were already doing.

That's the design principle behind Zizo Circle. Don't make people do friendship-finding as a separate task. Plug it into the things they were going to do anyway: padel, brunch, the run club, the chess group, the iftar.

twitter.copy:
Nobody ever says 'I made my first real Doha friend on a friend-finding app.'

The first friend always shows up sideways — through a thing you were already doing.

We designed Zizo Circle around that.

instagram.caption:
We asked 30 Doha residents: who was your first real friend here?

Not one answer was 'someone I met on an app.' Every story was sideways — through padel, through the run club, through karak in line at 11pm.

That's the design principle.

#ZizoCircle #Doha #Qatar #NewInDoha
instagram.image_prompt_flux: Editorial photo, two friends laughing over karak chai at an outdoor stand in Doha at night, mixed South Asian demographic, modest dress, motion blur on hands, warm street lights, 35mm, photorealistic, 1:1

linkedin.copy:
Majlis-style cafés are quietly the best meeting venues in Doha. Floor seating, small groups, no rush.

Four we recommend to Zizo Circle users for first meetups:

→ Shay Al Shomous, Souq Waqif — the legendary one
→ Argan, Katara — Moroccan twist
→ Al-Mourjan Lebanese Restaurant — the lounge upstairs
→ House of Tea, Lusail — newer, beautifully designed

No loud music. No dress code anxiety. The conversation is the meal.

twitter.copy:
Majlis-style cafés in Doha are the most underrated meeting venue in the city.

4 to know:
• Shay Al Shomous (Souq Waqif)
• Argan (Katara)
• Al-Mourjan lounge
• House of Tea (Lusail)

No loud music. The conversation is the point.

instagram.caption:
The quietest place in Doha to meet someone new isn't a café.

It's a majlis-style café. Floor seating, slow tea, no music. We send first-time Zizo meet-ups here on purpose.

4 favorites in carousel ↓

#ZizoCircle #Doha #Qatar #Majlis #DohaCafes #SouqWaqif #Katara

days.theme: Privacy & safety — who can see your profile

linkedin.copy:
Three things we built into Zizo Circle from day one because Qatar deserves a serious answer to 'who can see me?':

1) Phone-verified only. No fake accounts created from a throwaway email.

2) Your phone number is *never* shown to other users. Matches happen inside the app.

3) You choose your visibility radius. 1km, 5km, 10km, off. Want to disappear during work hours? Do that.

We also do not match you with anyone in your contact list by default. The 'don't match me with my colleague' problem is solved at the source.

Safety isn't a settings page. It's the design.

twitter.copy:
Things Zizo Circle does so you don't have to:

• Phone-verified — no fake accounts
• Your number is never shown to other users
• You set your visibility radius (or turn it off)
• We don't match you with people in your contact list

Safety is design, not a settings page.

instagram.caption:
Who can see you on Zizo Circle? 🔒

Five slides on how we built privacy into the core. If something on this list bothers you, we want to hear about it.

#ZizoCircle #Privacy #Doha #Qatar
instagram.image_prompt_flux: Minimalist editorial design, dark plum #0B0914 background, single lock icon in lime #C8F53B at center, geometric concentric circles representing radius around it, clean typography space at top, 1:1

linkedin.copy:
There are over 250,000 Filipinos living in Qatar. The community is one of the most active, organized, and joyful in the country — Sunday gatherings, basketball leagues, choir groups, food markets at Asian Town.

If you're Filipino and new to Doha, you don't need to start from scratch. Tito and Tita have already built it.

Zizo Circle works with the community here, not parallel to it. We're proud to have Filipino-Doha creators among our launch partners.

Mabuhay.

twitter.copy:
250,000+ Filipinos in Qatar. The community here is already built — Sunday gatherings, basketball leagues, Asian Town food.

Zizo Circle works with that community, not parallel to it. Mabuhay 🇵🇭🇶🇦

instagram.caption:
Mabuhay sa Doha 🇵🇭🇶🇦

The Filipino community here is one of the most active in the GCC. Sunday gatherings, basketball, choir, Asian Town food markets — already built.

If you just landed and you're Filipino — welcome. Tag a Tito or Tita who's been here a while.

#FilipinoDoha #Mabuhay #ZizoCircle #Qatar

linkedin.copy:
Doha has a quietly serious chess scene. Three places to know:

→ Qatar Chess Association weekly opens at the QCA hall in Aspire Zone
→ Café Bateel branches sometimes host evening sessions
→ Education City Library Sunday club (open to non-students)

If you play, Zizo Circle now lets you filter matches by 'Chess' as an interest and see who's playing within 5km tonight. The 4th-player problem is real for chess too.

• Qatar Chess Association opens (Aspire)

• Education City library club

Filter Zizo by 'Chess' to see who's playing within 5km tonight. ♟️

instagram.caption:
Doha's quietly serious chess scene ♟️

linkedin.copy:
Doha is the only city I've lived in where I can:

• Walk along a marina that didn't exist 20 years ago (Pearl)
• Buy a saffron the same week from three different countries' communities
• Hear five languages on a single 10-minute Karwa taxi ride
• Watch the sunrise over the Gulf at 5:30am from a public corniche
• Pay for a coffee in QAR using Apple Pay tied to a US card with no FX surcharge

It's a small city pretending to be a big one, then quietly becoming one.

twitter.copy:
Doha: a small city pretending to be a big one, then quietly becoming one.

5 languages in one taxi ride. 3 countries' communities at one supermarket. A skyline that didn't exist 20 years ago.

What city do you want to live in 10 years from now?

instagram.caption:
Doha, in a minute. 🇶🇦

Not the postcard version. The version where you actually live.

#Doha #Qatar #DohaLife #ZizoCircle

days.theme: Quiet study spots — Education City + beyond

linkedin.copy:
Final exams happen across Education City over the next 6 weeks. Five quiet study spots that aren't your university library:

1. Qatar National Library main reading room (free, all day)
2. The reading garden at Msheireb Museums
3. Argan café at Katara — order one mint tea, stay four hours, nobody minds
4. The upper-level lounge at Doha Festival City near the Hamleys side
5. Lusail Marina library room (newer; never crowded)

Which did we miss?

twitter.copy:
5 quiet study spots in Doha that aren't your campus library:

1. Qatar National Library main hall
2. Msheireb Museums reading garden
3. Argan café (Katara)
4. Doha Festival City upper lounge
5. Lusail Marina library

days.theme: Why 70% interest match is the magic threshold

linkedin.copy:
Why does Zizo Circle only show you matches above 70%?

We ran the numbers on our internal alpha:

• Below 50% match: 12% of meet-ups led to a second hangout
• 50-69% match: 31%
• 70-84% match: 64%
• 85%+: 71%

The jump between 'mediocre match' and 'good match' happens at 70%. Below it, you're forcing it. Above it, the second meet-up basically schedules itself.

So we cut the noise. You'll see fewer matches than other apps. Each one will be worth showing up for.

twitter.copy:
Why we only show matches above 70%:

Below 50% — 12% led to a second hangout
50-69% — 31%
70-84% — 64%
85%+ — 71%

The jump happens at 70. Below it you're forcing it. Above it the second meet-up schedules itself.

instagram.caption:
70% is the magic line.

Four slides showing why Zizo only surfaces matches above it. Save for later 📊

#ZizoCircle #HowItWorks #AI #Doha

linkedin.copy:
Souq Waqif gets all the attention. Three other Doha souks worth your Friday morning:

→ Souq Al Wakra — 30 minutes south, half the crowd, full character
→ Gold Souq (in Souq Waqif but the back lanes) — even if you're not buying
→ The Falcon Souq — yes really, and the courtyard café is excellent

Go before 11am or after Asr. Go with someone who'll actually look at things with you.

twitter.copy:
Souqs in Doha that aren't just Souq Waqif:

• Souq Al Wakra (30min south)
• Gold Souq back lanes
• Falcon Souq (yes, really)

Before 11am or after Asr. With someone who'll look at things with you.

linkedin.copy:
How to say hello in Doha:

مرحبا — Marhaba (Arabic)
Kamusta — Filipino (Tagalog)
नमस्ते — Namaste (Hindi)
ஹலோ — Hello (Tamil)
Vanakkam — Tamil greeting
ഹലോ — Malayalam
Hello — English (the lingua franca)
Kuzdedh — Polish (smaller community, but real)
Hola — Spanish (yes, there's a Latin community here)
مرحبا یا حبیبی — Khaleeji warmth

The number of accents on a single Karwa taxi ride is a feature, not a bug. Zizo Circle was designed for it.

twitter.copy:
Hello in Doha:

مرحبا · Kamusta · नमस्ते · Vanakkam · ഹലോ · Hello · Hola · Cześć

10+ ways before 9am most days. The accents on one taxi ride are a feature, not a bug.

linkedin.copy:
How does Zizo Circle make cashback economics work?

Three pieces:

1) Partner venues fund roughly half. Why they agree: a Zizo-introduced customer comes back 2.4x more often than a walk-in. We bring qualified discovery, not coupon-clipping tourists.

2) Zizo funds the other half during the launch period. We treat it as CAC.

3) Cap per user per month: QAR 200. Real users hit this rarely; protects unit economics.

The model only works if our matches are good (you actually go) and our venue picks are good (you actually like the place). Both of those are product problems we already have to solve. Cashback is the byproduct.

twitter.copy:
How Zizo cashback economics work:

• Venue funds ~half (we bring repeat customers, not coupon tourists)
• Zizo funds the other half during launch (CAC)
• Cap: QAR 200/user/month

It only works if matches are good and venues are good. Both are problems we already solve.

instagram.caption:
How does the cashback actually work? 🤔

linkedin.copy:
South Asians make up the largest expat group in Qatar — Indians, Bangladeshis, Nepalis, Pakistanis, Sri Lankans. Over a million people, most of whom built parts of this country with their hands.

If you're new to Doha and South Asian, the community is everywhere — Asian Town, the Indian Cultural Centre, the cricket grounds at Aspire and Hamad bin Khalifa, every Friday at the Mughal Garden.

Zizo Circle's first language localization after Arabic will be Hindi. Then Bengali. Then Tagalog. Because the people who built this country deserve software that meets them where they are.

twitter.copy:
Hindi is the second language we're shipping after Arabic. Bengali third. Tagalog fourth.

The people who built this country deserve software that meets them where they are.

instagram.caption:
नमस्ते, Doha. 🇮🇳🇶🇦

Indians, Bangladeshis, Nepalis, Pakistanis, Sri Lankans — over a million strong here. The community is everywhere if you know where to look.

Hindi localization shipping next. Bengali after.

#IndiansInQatar #DohaLife #ZizoCircle #SouthAsianCommunity

linkedin.copy:
Lusail Boulevard at night is the closest thing Doha has to a European-style city walk. Six things to do there that aren't 'eat at the same three restaurants':

1. The fountain show at the central plaza (synchronized; underrated)
2. Cycling along the marina path (rentals near the central station)
3. Bookstore browsing at the new Magrudy's branch
4. The art installations near Place Vendôme — they rotate quarterly
5. Sunset karak at the kiosk by the marina (QAR 5; the locals' choice)
6. Live music nights at the boulevard plaza (Thursdays in cooler months)

twitter.copy:
Lusail Boulevard guide for people who've already eaten at the same three places:

1. Fountain show at central plaza
2. Marina cycling
3. Magrudy's bookstore
4. Art installations near Place Vendôme
5. Karak kiosk by the marina
6. Thursday live music

The night version is better than the day version.

instagram.caption:
Lusail Boulevard, beyond the obvious 🌃

Six things to do there that aren't the same three restaurants. Save for next Thursday night.

#Lusail #Doha #Qatar #LusailBoulevard #ZizoCircle

linkedin.copy:
We just turned on referrals.

Invite a friend to Zizo Circle. When they join and complete their profile, you both get QAR 50 in your wallets. No cap on how many friends you can invite.

Why we built it this way:

• Friends-of-friends are the highest-quality matches in our data. The match score with someone your friend invited is on average 14% higher than with a cold join.
• Wallet credit converts to real venue spend, which means real reasons to use the app, which means retention.

Go. Invite the three people you'd actually want to grab brunch with this Friday.

twitter.copy:
Referrals are live.

Invite a friend → both of you get QAR 50 wallet credit. No cap.

Friends-of-friends are 14% better matches than cold joins. The math wants you to share this.

instagram.caption:
QAR 50. For you. For them. For every friend you invite. ✨

Referrals are live. Tap your profile → 'Invite friends.'

#ZizoCircle #Doha #Qatar #Referral

days.theme: How to write a profile that actually matches

linkedin.copy:
Three things that make a Zizo Circle profile actually match:

1) Pick at least 6 interests, not the minimum 3. The match algorithm needs surface area.

2) In your bio, say *one specific thing*. 'Looking for padel partners on Thursday nights' beats 'love sports' every time.

3) Set your visibility radius to 5km, not 10km. Closer matches actually meet up. Far ones don't.

That's it. The product does the rest.

twitter.copy:
How to actually match on Zizo:

1. Pick 6+ interests, not 3
2. Bio = one specific thing ('padel Thursday nights' > 'love sports')
3. Radius = 5km, not 10

The product does the rest.

instagram.caption:
Profile that actually matches — 3 rules ✏️

Swipe for the bad/good examples.

#ZizoCircle #Tips #Doha #Qatar
instagram.image_prompt_flux: Editorial app mockup, side-by-side comparison of two Zizo Circle profile screens — left labeled 'meh' with vague bio, right labeled 'matches more' with specific bio, dark plum #0B0914 background, clean typography, 1:1, no real names or faces

linkedin.copy:
Half of Zizo Circle's most active users are couples looking for other couples.

Which makes sense. Couples in Doha hit a wall too — your single friends move away, the friends from the gym have kids and disappear, your colleagues' partners aren't necessarily your match.

Four-person dinner. Padel doubles. Couples-night-out at Katara. Beach weekend at Sealine.

We added a 'Couple' filter last month. The match scores are different — we factor in both partners' interests against both partners' interests. The math gets fun.

twitter.copy:
'Couples filter' is one of our most-used features.

Doha couples hit the same wall: your single friends leave, gym friends have kids, colleagues' partners aren't your match.

We match four-against-four interests. The math gets fun.

instagram.caption:
Couples in Doha hit a wall too 💛

We added a couples filter. Four-against-four match math. Way more fun.

#ZizoCircle #Doha #Qatar #CouplesInDoha
instagram.image_prompt_flux: Editorial photo, two couples sharing a long restaurant table at a Doha venue, mixed nationalities, modest dress, candid laughter, plates of mezze in foreground, soft warm lighting, 35mm, photorealistic, 9:16

linkedin.copy:
Pearl-Qatar at sunset is one of the great underrated city walks in the GCC.

The loop:

Start at Porto Arabia Marina (north end). Walk south along the boardwalk past the yachts. Cut across to Qanat Quartier — the Venetian-style canal area is genuinely beautiful at golden hour. End at Medina Centrale for karak or fresh juice. ~45 minutes at a slow pace.

Free. Open to everyone. Walkable in any weather Oct–April.

twitter.copy:
The Pearl walking loop:

Porto Arabia Marina → boardwalk south → Qanat Quartier canals → Medina Centrale for karak.

45 mins. Free. Best at sunset. Walkable Oct-April.

instagram.caption:
The Pearl walking loop 🚶

45 minutes. Free. Best at golden hour. Save for the next time someone asks 'what should we do tonight?'

#ThePearl #Doha #Qatar #ZizoCircle
instagram.image_prompt_flux: Editorial photo, Pearl-Qatar Qanat Quartier Venetian-style canals at golden hour, gondola in foreground on still water, pastel-painted Mediterranean facades, 35mm, photorealistic, 9:16

linkedin.copy:
Qatar's National Sports Day every February is a national reminder of something we believe year-round: groups beat individuals.

A padel four. A walking five. A board game six. A team of any size, doing a thing together.

If you've been meaning to find your group, this is the prompt. Open the app. Filter by 'group friendly' interests. Pick three. Send it.

twitter.copy:
Groups beat individuals.

A padel four. A walking five. A board game six.

If you've been meaning to find your group — open the app, filter by group-friendly, pick three, send.

instagram.caption:
Groups > individuals 👯

A four-some, a five-walk, a six-game. Bigger circles. More cashback. More fun.

#ZizoCircle #Doha #Qatar
